Grand Central Hotel - Lobby.name: Grand Central Hotel - Lobby
Grand Central Hotel - Lobby.description: The kind of lobby that used to be grand. Marble floors worn smooth. A chandelier that needs three bulbs replaced. The front desk has one person working it - the person you came here for.
Grand Central Hotel - Lobby.first_entry_text:
The lobby is quiet for early evening. A few guests with rolling suitcases. Elevator music that somehow makes everything worse.

The receptionist looks up as you walk in. They tilt their head, curious. You look like someone with a problem.

furniture.name: Front Desk
furniture.description: A heavy oak reception desk. Behind it, a computer, a phone, and a row of key hooks.

objects.name: Chandelier
objects.description: The chandelier above the lobby flickers once. You look up. Three bulbs are out.

objects.name: Guest Register
objects.description: An old-fashioned paper register on the desk. The last name signed in today is one you recognize.

Hotel Bar.name: Hotel Bar
Hotel Bar.description: A dark wood bar with six stools, three of them occupied. The bartender is also the only person listening to you if you decide to talk. The bottles behind the bar are lit from below, casting everything in amber.

objects.name: Bar Mirror
objects.description: A long mirror behind the bar. You catch your own reflection. You look worse than you thought.

objects.name: Bar Radio
objects.description: A small radio playing jazz. It skips once, and for a moment you hear a different station - something that sounds like a news report about a fire.

Hotel Elevator.name: Hotel Elevator
Hotel Elevator.description: A small elevator with brushed steel doors. The floor indicator stops at F2 for a long time before descending to lobby.
Hotel Elevator.first_entry_text: You press the button. The elevator hums. Somewhere above you, something shifts in the shaft.

objects.name: Control Panel
objects.description: Lobby, 2, 3, Emergency Stop. The button panel is cracked on the corner.

objects.name: Emergency Phone
objects.description: A small red phone behind a glass panel. For use in case of emergency. The glass is already broken.

Hotel Corridor - Floor 1.name: Hotel Corridor - Floor 1
Hotel Corridor - Floor 1.description: A long carpeted corridor with numbered doors on either side. The carpet pattern is aggressive. One of the room doors is ajar and light spills out.

objects.name: Ajar Door
objects.description: Room 104. The TV inside is playing something. The sound is wrong - not dialogue, just a steady tone.

objects.name: Ice Machine
objects.description: The ice machine at the end of the corridor cycles on with a clunk. Ice clatters into the bin.

Hotel Corridor - Floor 2.name: Hotel Corridor - Floor 2
Hotel Corridor - Floor 2.description: Identical to floor 1 except for room numbers and a water stain shaped like nothing in particular on the ceiling. One of the room doors at the far end is chained shut from the outside.

objects.name: Ceiling Fan
objects.description: The ceiling fan wobbles on its mount. It should not wobble that much.

objects.name: Chained Door
objects.description: Room 217. Chained from the outside. A DO NOT DISTURB sign hangs on the knob anyway.

Stairwell Access.name: Stairwell Access
Stairwell Access.description: A utilitarian stairwell connecting floors. The walls are painted cinder block. A fire hose cabinet on the wall. Emergency exit signs point down.

objects.name: Fire Hose Cabinet
objects.description: The cabinet is closed but not latched. The hose inside is dry and brittle.

objects.name: Emergency Exit Sign
objects.description: Pointing down. Someone has drawn an arrow on the wall pointing up with the words BUT ALSO THIS WAY.
